summary: Command monitoring testing reveals bugs
description:
Bugs discovered:
* bulk_write method not propagating write concern
* query limit not propagated into QueryResult for command form (not strictly necessary, as the server should handle it, but this is better for consistency)
* 'num' field of cursors not incremented correctly.

summary: prefer_numeric interprets dual vars as strings, not numbers
description: Dual vars (PVIV and PVNV) are being interpreted as strings due to the presence of the POK flag.  The MongoDB driver historically used IOK/NOK first before acting on the prefer_numeric config option.  There was no coverage of this expectation so we need tests and a fix.

summary: BSON::XS - prefer_numeric interprets dual vars as strings, not numbers
description: Dual vars (PVIV and PVNV) are being interpreted as strings due to the presence of the POK flag.  The MongoDB driver historically used IOK/NOK first before acting on the prefer_numeric config option.  There was no coverage of this expectation so we need tests and a fix.

summary: Tests fail when BSON.pm type wrappers replace MongoDB.pm type wrappers
Replace wire version checks with feature discovery methods
description: Many places in the code repeatedly call {{$link->accepts_wire_version(4)}}.  We should replace those with a feature method that is computed once when processing isMaster metadata.

summary: Decoding BSON::DBRef and BSON::Code leaks memory
description: Found via the {{xt/leaktest.t}} file.

summary: New version of Type::Tiny::XS has stricter definition of type Bool
description: The latest Type::Tiny::XS tightens the definition of the "Bool" type such that boolean.pm objects are no longer allowed.  This breaks a bunch of our validation if assertions are on.

summary: Broken BSON::Timestamp back compatible constructor
description: To be backwards compatible with MongoDB::Timestamp, BSON::Timestamp needs to look for a 'sec' constructor argument, not 'secs'.

summary: BSON::String in boolean context gives non-numeric string warnings
description: The auto-generated boolean operator appears to rely on the numification operator.  We need to implement a boolean overload that avoids numification.

summary: Drop dependency on DateTime module
description:
The DateTime module is heavy and slow and we want to avoid it being the default for inflated BSON datetime fields in v2.0.0.

If we can drop it entirely from our deep dependency chain, then we can cut out several dependencies, some of which have been seen to be flaky from time to time and give users trouble installing.

Users who want DateTime inflation will be able to do so via the BSON::Date methods to do inflation on demand if the module is available.

summary: Rewrite MongoDB::DataTypes documentation
description: The {{MongoDB::DataTypes}} POD needs to be rewritten to reflect the new use of BSON type wrapper and the new encoder features.

summary: Little spelling error
description:
Hello,

just a little spelling error

summary: Conditionally require BSON::XS if a compiler is available
description:
In the Makefile.PL if a compiler is available, we should require BSON::XS in addition to BSON.

This should wait until after PERL-666.

summary: user/password encoding restrictions validate incorrectly
description:
The following two bugs were observed:
* use of '%' in username or password when not percent escaping was allowed but should have been prohibited
* use of subdelimiters should be allowed (per [RFC 3986 § 3.2.1|https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3986#section-3.2.1]) but was prohibited

summary: Topology type detection regression
description: PERL-793 changed the default topology type to allow discovery of standalone servers or mongos.  However, this introduces a breaking change making direct connections to replica set members.

summary: t/sessions-end.t fails against multiple mongoses
description: The test queries a server for the local session it has, but this number is not global across all mongoses -- it also could fail when run in parallel and other processes have established sessions.

summary: Skip maxScan tests when server is 4.1+
BSON::XS memory leaks and early free
description:
From inspection two errors were observed:

* cycle detection is freeing the detection stack too early in the case of a subdocument
* several calls to {{call_perl_reader}} do not mortalize the return value

summary: BSON::Doc encoding occasionally corrupts the stack
description: Nested BSON::Doc encoding occasionally seems to corrupt the stack on Perl before 5.26.  I haven't been able to replicate this except on Evergreen, but suspect the issue is in the XS code that calls the BSON::Doc iterator callback.

summary: Authen::SCRAM dependency sometimes fails tests
description: Evergreen dependency builds sometimes fail due to fatalized test warnings originating in a deep dependency (String::Compare::ConstantTime).  There is a newer version of Authen::SCRAM that temporarily drops that recommended dependency.

summary: Create v2 Upgrading documentation
description: The existing MongoDB::Upgrading document needs to move to MongoDB::Upgrading::V1 and we need a new MongoDB::Upgrading::V2 document to accumulate details of what's deprecated and removed since v1.

summary: 1.999.0 fails max_staleness_spec.t on 32-bit perls
description: CPAN Testers has a number of failures for 1.999.0.  The [failure analysis page|http://analysis.cpantesters.org/solved?distv=MongoDB-v1.999.0-TRIAL] correlates the failing tests to 32-bit integers.

summary: SDAM does not gossip $clusterTime
Command Monitoring spec tests should use the same format for bulk writes
description:
There are three changes, the first two are breaking. 

The two breaking changes are in three command-monitoring tests: "bulkWrite.yml", "unacknowledgedBulkWrite.yml" and "insertMany.yml":

https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/bebf9b688f64afdec46a860f102bad0841b04c02

The format of the "requests" array and "ordered" option changes from this:
{noformat}
    operation:
      name: "bulkWrite"
      arguments:
        requests:
          - insertOne: 
              document: { _id: 4, x: 44 }
        ordered: false
{noformat}
To this:
{noformat}
    operation:
      name: "bulkWrite"
      arguments:
        requests:
          - name: "insertOne"
            arguments:
              document: { _id: 4, x: 44 }
        options:
          ordered: false
{noformat}
This brings Command Monitoring tests in line with the Retryable Writes and forthcoming Transactions tests.

The non-breaking change is in the Retryable Writes tests:

https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/c0ce96daac5661f2603bfa52c9b3f683c176a292

Before, the tests said in prose that all MongoClients should be created with retryWrites=true. Now, the tests include the following YAML:
{noformat}
        clientOptions:
            retryWrites: true
{noformat}
The clientOptions are the same for all Retryable Writes tests. Interpreting this field now will prepare you for the Transactions tests, which make real use of the field.

summary: Update ChangeStream spec to detail usage of startAtOperationTime
description:
If drivers have already implemented DRIVERS-464, they need to update their implementation of how they initially store {{startAtOperationTime}}. Instead of setting a default {{startAtOperationTime}} when the ChangeStream is created, drivers must omit a default {{startAtOperationTime}}. When resuming, drivers must use the {{operationTime}} of the initial aggregate command as the default {{startAtOperationTime}} value.

See https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/745debcf4624ec820eb9b138cd8f28fa7b1e5782 for the exact implementation details.

summary: Change streams don't support explicit sessions
Resync retryable writes tests
description: Resync retryable writes spec tests.

summary: Circular reference detected
description:
Release testing flagged a circular reference problem

{noformat}
# #   Failed test at xt/author/circular-refs.t line 36.
# Cycle #1
#     MongoDB::MongoClient A->{_credential} => MongoDB::_Credential B
#     MongoDB::_Credential B->{_scram_sha256_client} => Authen::SCRAM::Client C
#     Authen::SCRAM::Client C->{_nonce_generator} => &D
#     closure &D, $self => $E
#     $E => Authen::SCRAM::Client C
# Cycle #2
#     MongoDB::MongoClient A->{_server_session_pool} => MongoDB::_SessionPool F
#     MongoDB::_SessionPool F->{client} => MongoDB::MongoClient A
# Cycle #3
#     MongoDB::MongoClient A->{_topology} => MongoDB::_Topology G
#     MongoDB::_Topology G->{credential} => MongoDB::_Credential B
#     MongoDB::_Credential B->{_scram_sha256_client} => Authen::SCRAM::Client C
#     Authen::SCRAM::Client C->{_nonce_generator} => &D
#     closure &D, $self => $E
#     $E => Authen::SCRAM::Client C
{noformat}

summary: t/threads/cursor.t errors in global destruction
description:
Tested against a 4.0 replica set with auth enabled on darwin:

{noformat}
panic: del_backref, *svp=0 phase=RUN refcnt=0 at t/threads/cursor.t line 111.
# Tests were run but no plan was declared and done_testing() was not seen.
# Looks like your test exited with 255 just after 2.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "cluster_time" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "options" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "read_concern_level" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "read_pref_mode" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "password" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "username" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "_cursor_id" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "max_staleness_seconds" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "_uri" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "read_pref_tag_sets" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "bson_codec" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "_id" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "_transaction_state" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "auth_mechanism_properties" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "_server_session_pool" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "socket_check_interval_ms" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "host" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "socket_timeout_ms" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "operation_time" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "_deferred" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "_write_concern" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "connect_timeout_ms" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "_credential" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "heartbeat_frequency_ms" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "replica_set_name" during global destruction.
Unbalanced string table refcount: (1) for "zlib_compression_level" during global destruction.
Scalars leaked: 6
t/threads/cursor.t ......................... Dubious, test returned 255 (wstat 65280, 0xff00)
{noformat}

Does not occur with a standalone (or is intermittent), so this may be related to implicit sessions.

summary: Provide Transactions example for Docs
Add 'client' method to Database
description: Now that users need easy access to MongoClient for creating sessions, we should add a public 'client' method to Database, just like we have for Collection.

summary: Expand docs on Transactions in ClientSession
t/cursor.t occasionally fails away cursor test
description: Example: https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task_log_raw/perl_driver_os_ubuntu1604_perl_24_test_3.4_RS_069f556c9cd6a78ed81b08d5a5094a3fe2e31870_18_06_25_16_08_38/0?type=T

summary: Document how to avoid shadows on installation
description:
Because of the change to pure-Perl, the installation directory has changed and installing over an old version may leave the old version earlier in the {{@INC}} path than the new version.

The upgrading document should show users how to avoid this problem.

summary: Use of default compression level warns
BSON/BSON::XS tests warn about deprecated MongoDB classes
BSON tests non-existing MongoDB::BSON::_EncodedDoc
description: t/mapping/hashref.t tries to test MongoDB::BSON::_EncodedDoc that was removed from MongoDB-v2.0.0. I think it's time to remove the dead code from the test.

summary: BSON::XS fails on old perls due to missing pthread_once
description: Seen in Travis CI: https://travis-ci.org/mongodb/mongo-perl-bson-xs/jobs/397825308

summary: Private constructor role doesn't reify deferred sub generation
description: Moo defers installing destructors until {{new}} is first called.  Our private constructor doesn't do that so destructors aren't called.  We need to ensure Moo's expected generation happens, even when bypassing the normal constructor.

summary: Don't require BSON::XS on Windows XP and earlier
Document that X509 doesn't need username
description: After PERL-679, the driver no longer needs a username with X509, but this is not clearly documented.

summary: Spelling errors
description:
Hi,
some new errors reported by Lintian.

summary: BSON::XS only supports Windows vista or later
description: BSON::XS needs Windows vista.  On Windows XP, BSON::XS should refuse to build and MongoDB should not include BSON:XS as a dependency, even when a compiler is present.

summary: Amend transaction examples
description: PR: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-perl-driver/pull/172/files#diff-0

summary: GridFS DownloadStream warns in spec tests on old perls
BSON::XS must not interpret top level $ref key as DBRef
Document that TCP keepalive defaults to true
description:
The Perl driver already sets this by default and relies on system settings for intervals, etc.

The work for this ticket is to document this behavior (or confirm existing documentation).

summary: Can't use BSON::Doc as sort option in find command
description: Passing BSON::Doc as a sort option errors complaining it can't be converted to Tie::IxHash.

summary: Index lists should preserve key order in results
Ensure hint used with aggregate is string or IxHash or BSON::Doc
description: We should make sure wherever we take a hint field, that it's a string or "ordered document" – and in the latter case, we should coerce an array to IxHash as the database won't take an array for a hint.
